---
title: "Bias Mitigation Pipeline in AI-Driven Hiring Processes"
author: "Muhammad Ahmad (19166-MA1266)"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: ioslides_presentation
---

## Title

Bias Mitigation Pipeline in AI-Driven Hiring Processes  
R-Based DALC/MLOps Implementation

## Problem Statement

- AI hiring can encode historical bias
- Protected attribute: **Gender**
- Goal: reduce unfair disparity while preserving utility

## Motivation (2026 Context)

- Ethical AI and trust requirements are rising
- Hiring systems affect livelihoods at scale
- Need explainable, auditable mitigation workflows

## Literature Highlights

- Bias mitigation with XAI in hiring [1]
- Fairness-oriented resume screening [2]
- Framework and governance updates [3]
- Foundational fairness theory [5]-[12]

## DALC/MLOps Methodology

1. Data Preparation
2. EDA & Bias Detection
3. Modeling + Reweighting
4. Fairness Evaluation
5. XAI
6. Deployment API

## Dataset Overview

- Source: `data/final_bias_hiring_dataset.csv`
- Features: education, experience, skills, interview
- Target: `Final_Decision` (Selected/Rejected)
- Protected attribute: `Gender` (Male/Female)

## EDA Findings

- Group-level selection rates differ by gender
- Score distributions are close but not identical
- Correlation shows interview and skills drive outcomes

## Baseline Model

- Logistic regression without mitigation
- Trained via `tidymodels`
- Used as disparity reference point

## Mitigation Strategy

- Reweight underrepresented Gender × Decision combinations
- Train weighted Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost

## Model Performance Comparison

- Accuracy, AUC, F1 compared across all models
- Focus on fairness-performance trade-off

## Fairness Metrics Before/After

- Demographic Parity Difference
- Equalized Odds Difference
- Disparate Impact Ratio
- Statistical Parity

## XAI Findings

- DALEX feature importance
- Break-down attribution for individual applicants
- Partial dependence for key features

## Error Analysis

- Confusion matrices by gender
- FPR/FNR comparison before and after mitigation
- Bias reduction target: 30-40%

## Live Demo Overview

- Train and save model (`R/save_model.R`)
- Start Plumber API (`api/plumber.R`)
- Test `/health`, `/predict`, `/fairness_report`

## UAE Relevance

- Supports inclusive digital HR practices
- Aligns with responsible AI adoption goals
- Improves transparency for decision support

## Limitations & Future Work

- Synthetic dataset constraints
- Single protected attribute
- Future: intersectional fairness + monitoring

## Conclusion

- End-to-end reproducible pipeline delivered
- Fairness mitigation integrated with explainability
- Deployment-ready through Plumber API

## References

[1]-[12] as listed in the IEEE paper.

## Q&A

Thank you.
